rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALZINA MARIE RAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed at hearing: 1) No notice of hearing for the second amendment filed July 29, 2024, has been filed. An amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends (CRC 7.53(a)). 2) Court to determine whether to distribute Marcus McClure's distributive share to his trustee before appropriate bond has been posted. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHERINE S. KIM
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/23/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] The petition does not set forth how much more time the administrator is requesting. (The Notice of Remote Appearance is not required in Probate cases and is not notice to an interested party about how the interested party may appear remotely.) Please provide a courtesy copy of the correct, complete proof of service of notice of the 10/23/24 hearing to the assigned examiner by 10/14/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Not providing courtesy copies and a proposed order, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E., can delay review. All pleadings must show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RYAN TIMOTHY HODGSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/21/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) It does not appear that anyone has been appointed to represent the interests of the minors. No guardian ad litem was appointed at the outset of the proceedings or a guardian of the estate to receive distributions from the testamentary trust. (2) The decedent's final income tax return has not yet been filed, and the federal estate tax return was not expected to be filed until after the petition was filed. Please address how taxes will be paid and what the amount of taxes are. [LRSF 14.35 I.1.) (3) Executor alleges that he took no actions under his IAEA authority. What happened to the two vehicles? (4) This Court actually requires time sheets supporting any request for fees for extraordinary services. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/11/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TORU SAKAMOTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/21/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) There is no attachment to the proposed order. (2) The incorrect form of notice was served. (3) The proof of service filed on 7/18/24 was incomplete at items 4a (date mailed) and 4b (state), and was purportedly verified on 8/18/24. Please provide a complete proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/11/24. (Supplements or amendments to a petition should just provide additional or different information, as appropriate, not "respond" to issues posted in a tentative ruling like a test.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JACOB TICE POWELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 4/12/24. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set for 8/25/25. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 8/25/25, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST B-1 AND TRUST B-2 ESTABLISHED UNDER THE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order NB: (1) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] (2) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) It is unclear why an entire copy of the petition was attached to each Waiver of Notice and Consent; this was entirely unnecessary and wasteful. (4) The settlement agreement does not actually require Court approval; however, the date that the trust was amended and restated was 8/7/96, not 6/17/98. The first amendment to the restated trust was dated 6/17/98.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALIAKSANDR KRUKAU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/23/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: An ex-spouse does not have priority before the public administrator unless she was appointed as the guardian of the minor's estate. Please file a copy of letters (or the Michigan equivalent thereof), certified within 60 days. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address this issue to the assigned examiner by 10/14/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
